name: Pork Chops with Raspberry Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Put the raspberry jam in a small glass measuring cup. Microwave at HIGH for about 20-30 seconds or until melted. Strain to remove seeds. Stir in the vinegar and set aside.
2. Lightly sprinkle both sides of the pork chops with salt and pepper. In a large, heavy frypan, heat the butter and oil over medium-high heat. Add the chops and sauté for about 3-1/2 minutes or until cooked, turning once. Transfer the chops to a serving platter; keep warm.
3. Drain off all but 1 tablespoon of fat from the frypan. Reduce the heat to medium-low and add the shallot to the frypan. Sauté for about 1 minute. Stir in the stock and boil for about 2 minutes or until slightly reduced, stirring and scraping browned bits from the bottom of the pan.
4. Drain any juices from the pork chops into the frypan. Stir in the raspberry jam mixture, simmering for 2-3 minutes or until slightly thickened. Add salt and pepper to taste. Pour over the pork chops.
